issues.description: Chapter numbering inconsistency - this is labeled as 'CHAPTER I' but should be Chapter 2 according to the story bible timeline and character introductions
issues.suggestion: Update chapter heading to reflect correct chapter number in sequence

issues.description: Extended digression about Robinson Crusoe interrupts narrative momentum and delays story progression
issues.suggestion: Condense the Robinson Crusoe exposition to maintain forward momentum while preserving Betteredge's character voice

issues.description: Chapter ends without advancing the mystery or planting new clues, focusing instead on narrative setup
issues.original_text: Still, this don't look much like starting the story of the Diamond—does it? I seem to be wandering off in search of Lord knows what, Lord knows where. We will take a new sheet of paper, if you please, and begin over again, with my best respects to you.
issues.suggestion: Add a hint about the mystery or a teaser about what's to come to better serve mystery genre expectations

pacing.chapter_summary: Gabriel Betteredge recounts his conversation with Mr. Franklin Blake about writing the story of the Moonstone diamond. Franklin explains that multiple people will contribute their perspectives to create a complete record that will clear innocent people who suffered under suspicion. Betteredge reluctantly agrees to write his portion but then spends considerable time discussing his devotion to Robinson Crusoe before admitting he's been procrastinating and needs to start over with actually telling the Diamond's story.

conventions_met: Establishes collaborative investigation method through multiple narrators
References the central mystery (loss of the Diamond)
Mentions innocent people under suspicion, expanding suspect pool concept
conventions_missed: No new clues planted in this chapter
No progression of the central mystery investigation
Missing atmospheric details that could serve the mystery
No new questions raised about the crime itself
